tests.name: Count met: eliminate remaining matches

Count saturation: vowel count met
Only one remaining option → force

OnlySameOtherMatch: Q3=B already, so Q1 option B (pointing to Q2) can't be 'only'
ConsecIdent: one answered, other has that answer eliminated → eliminate
ConsecIdent: disjoint possible answers → eliminate
ConsecIdent: overlapping possible answers → no elimination
LetterDist: all valid target answers eliminated → eliminate
LetterDist: both valid target answers eliminated → eliminate
LetterDist: one valid target answer remains → no elimination
LetterDist: target not answered → no elimination
FirstWith: claims None, no answer in range → no elimination
PrevSame: valid claim, no closer match → no elimination
ConsecIdent: pair unanswered → no elimination
Counting elim: option value matches → no elimination
Count saturation: count_answer_before range
Count saturation: consonant count met
LetterDist: multiple valid options → no force
LetterDist: target not answered → no force
Counting force: count_answer with remaining=0
Count sat: count_answer_after range
Count sat: no saturation when count between 0 and value
Count sat: matching option already eliminated → skip to next
Forced: LetterDist with 2 remaining but only 1 valid → force
Forced: LetterDist zero valid options → no force
Forced: counting with count_vowel remaining=0
Vowel/consonant: all complements exist → no elimination
Vowel/consonant: one answered → skip (only unanswered pairs)

Counting elim: count_answer_after option impossible

LetterDist: multiple options wrong, first eliminated

OnlyOdd: claims None, no odd match → no elimination
ConsecIdent: one answered, other has answer eliminated → eliminate
ConsecIdent: both unanswered, overlapping → no elimination
ConsecIdent: claims None, no pair → no elimination
ConsecIdent: self-ref, answer eliminated from partner → eliminate
LetterDist: impossible distance for option letter → eliminate
PrevSame: None value doesn't trigger elimination
NextSame: None value doesn't trigger elimination
NextSame: answer eliminated from target → eliminate
OnlySame: answer eliminated from target, other options valid → selective
SameAs: answer eliminated from target → eliminate
SameAs: answer not eliminated from target → no elimination
FirstWith: answered, eliminate answer from earlier positions
LastWith: answered, eliminate answer from later positions
NextSame: answered, eliminate own answer from questions in between
PrevSame: answered, eliminate own answer from questions in between
FirstWith: unanswered, min remaining option excludes earlier positions
LastWith: unanswered, max remaining option excludes later positions
FirstWith: no elimination when answer already eliminated
CountMustMatchForce: exactly one position remaining must be A
CountExceeded: more A's answered than option claims → eliminate
PrevNextOnlySameReverse: PrevSame answered, forces target question
LetterDistReverseForce: answered LetterDist src forces target to one option
LetterDistReverseElim: answered LetterDist src eliminates incompatible target options

UniqueAlreadyUsed: not yet implemented → no elimination

LeastCommon: claimed letter must be uniquely least → force
LeastCommonForce unsound: both Q10=A and Q10=E self-consistent
MostCommon: claimed letter's max < another letter's min → eliminate
MostCommon: claimed letter could still be most → no elimination
MostCommon: claimed letter's min >= all others' max → force
MostCommon: claimed letter could be overtaken → no force
MostCommon: tie still possible with another letter → no force
OnlyOdd: remaining options exclude other odd positions → eliminate
TrueStatement: answered, claim is last_with_answer → force target
TrueStatementMatchForce: CountVowel question = 2 forces the 'vowels == 2' claim
TrueStatementMatchForce: selecting the 'vowels == 2' claim forces the CountVowel question to 2
TrueStatementMatchElim: AnswerOf(4) question = A refutes the 'AnswerOf(4) == C' claim
TrueStatementMatchElim: ruling out the 'AnswerOf(4) == C' claim rules C out of the AnswerOf(4) question
TrueStatementMatchForce: CountConsonant question = 3 (n=4) forces the complement 'vowels == 1' claim
All answered → null
Rule filter: CountSaturated skips OnlyOptionLeft
Deduce all: finds CountSaturated first
PrevSameNoneMatch: None claims no previous B, but Q1=B → eliminate
NextSameNoneMatch: None claims no next B, but Q3=B → eliminate
OnlySameNoneMatch: None claims unique answer, but Q3=B → eliminate B
OnlySameNoneForward: Q2=B (None), so eliminate B from others
ConsecIdentForwardForce: pair 2-3 answered, Q3=C → force Q4=C
ConsecIdentForwardElim: pair 2-3, Q3 has C eliminated → eliminate Q4 C
ConsecIdentForwardBothForce: pair 2-3, only B in common → force Q3=B
ConsecIdentReverse: only pairs 1-2 and 2-3 possible, Q5=B → eliminate Q4 B
TrueStatementSelfRef: FirstWith claims self has answer A, but option is C
SameAsWhichForward: ref answered, option target has wrong answer → eliminate
SameAsWhichForward: target unanswered but ref answer eliminated → eliminate
SameAsWhichReverse: answered, ref known → force option target
SameAsWhichReverse: answered, option target known → force ref
TrueStatementClaimInvalid: FirstWith claims Q2 has answer A, but Q2=C
TrueStatementClaimInvalid: NoOtherHasAnswer claims B is unique, but Q3=B and Q4=B → eliminate
TrueStatementClaimValid: only one claim still possible → force
TrueStatementClaimKnownTrue: one claim is already provably true → force
TrueStatementClaimKnownTrue: self-referencing AnswerOf must NOT trigger force
